itin_education.hero_body_prefix: An ITIN is a tax ID number issued by the IRS. It lets you pay taxes and access financial services —
itin_education.benefit_available: Available to anyone, regardless of immigration status
itin_education.benefit_irs_no_share: IRS doesn't share info with immigration authorities
itin_education.benefit_history: Helps you build a financial history in the US
itin_education.benefit_credit: Can help you get credit cards and loans
itin_education.benefit_property: Needed for buying property or starting a business
itin_education.privacy_title: Your privacy is protected by law
itin_education.privacy_body_suffix: from sharing your information with immigration agencies (Section 6103 of the Internal Revenue Code). Getting an ITIN does not affect your immigration status.
itin_education.step_3_desc: By mail or through an agent
itin_education.btn_skip: I'll do this later — continue with limited features

itin_application_help.header_subtitle: We'll guide you through the process
itin_application_help.option_caa_desc: Faster, easier, and you keep your passport. We'll help you find one near you.
itin_application_help.option_mail_desc: Fill out Form W-7 and mail it with your original documents to the IRS.
itin_application_help.faq_time_q: How long does it take to get an ITIN?
itin_application_help.faq_time_a: Usually 7-11 weeks if you apply by mail. If you use a Certified Acceptance Agent (CAA), it can be faster and you don't have to mail your original documents.
itin_application_help.faq_mail_q: Do I need to mail my original passport?
itin_application_help.faq_mail_a: If you apply by mail directly to IRS, yes. But if you use a Certified Acceptance Agent, they can verify your documents in person and you keep your passport.
itin_application_help.faq_cost_a: The IRS doesn't charge for ITINs. However, Certified Acceptance Agents may charge $50-$100 for their services. We recommend using a CAA for convenience.
itin_application_help.faq_immigration_q: Will this affect my immigration status?
itin_application_help.faq_immigration_a: No. The IRS is legally prohibited from sharing your information with immigration agencies. Getting an ITIN has no effect on your immigration status.
itin_application_help.btn_skip: I'll apply later — continue with limited features

itin_pending.interest_growing_body: You'll unlock it as soon as your ITIN is verified.
itin_pending.wait_title: While you wait, you can still:
itin_pending.wait_join: Join and contribute to savings circles
itin_pending.wait_reputation: Build your reputation in the community
itin_pending.faq_time_a: Usually 7-11 weeks if submitted by mail. Using a Certified Acceptance Agent can sometimes be faster.
itin_pending.faq_notify_q: How will I know when it's approved?
itin_pending.faq_notify_a: The IRS will mail you a letter with your ITIN. We'll also notify you in the app as soon as you add it.
itin_pending.faq_track_a: Unfortunately, the IRS doesn't provide online tracking. You can call 1-800-829-1040 after 7 weeks to check status.

help.title: About this trip
help.topic_status_body: Confirmed means your spot is secured — the deposit landed and the organizer added you. Pending means you've registered but haven't paid your deposit yet, so the spot isn't held. Waitlist means the trip is full but you'll be promoted automatically if someone drops. Cancelled means your spot was released — contact the organizer if this is unexpected.
help.topic_payment_body: Most trips ask for a deposit upfront to lock your spot, followed by installments leading up to departure, with the balance due before the trip. If you miss a due date, you'll see an overdue warning. Two consecutive missed payments can result in your spot being released — pay as soon as you can or message the organizer.
help.topic_documents_body: Each trip may require documents like a passport copy, emergency contact, or a signed liability waiver. Tap any row in your checklist to upload. The organizer reviews each submission before it shows as verified.
help.topic_refunds_body: Refund rules are set by each trip's organizer and are visible on the public trip page before you join. If you need to cancel, message the organizer first. Refunds (when offered) are processed back to your original payment method and may take a few business days to appear.

participant_manager.help_payment_statuses_body: Unpaid — no payment yet. Deposit paid — partial payment received. Paid in full — full balance settled. Refunded — money returned to the participant.
participant_manager.help_what_tap_does_body: Tap any row to open the full participant detail screen with payment history, documents, and confirm/cancel actions.
participant_manager.help_cancellations_body: Cancelled participants move to the Cancelled tab. They can't rejoin the trip themselves — re-invite them or they'll need to re-register from scratch.

participant_detail.confirm_body: This marks the participant as confirmed for the trip.
participant_detail.cancel_body: This removes the participant from the trip. They may need a refund if they've already paid.
participant_detail.help_confirm_vs_cancel_body: Confirm marks the participant as confirmed for your trip and notifies them. Cancel removes their spot and notifies them. Both actions are reflected immediately for everyone.
participant_detail.help_refunds_body: Cancelling does not automatically refund a participant. If they've already paid you'll need to handle the refund manually via Stripe.
participant_detail.help_doc_verification_body: Tap a document to view and mark it as verified. The participant sees this status update on their trip status screen.
participant_detail.help_payment_timeline_body: Every successful charge appears under Payments. When the full balance is paid, the participant's status auto-promotes to Paid in full.

trip_dashboard.empty_body: Share your trip to get the first booking.
trip_dashboard.help_stats_body: Confirmed — paid and confirmed for the trip. Pending — registered but haven't paid the deposit yet. Waitlist — trip is full; they'll be promoted when a spot opens.
trip_dashboard.help_lifecycle_body: Each participant moves: Pending → (after deposit) Confirmed → (after full payment) Paid in full → Completed when the trip wraps.
trip_dashboard.help_quick_actions_body: Participants — manage the roster. Itinerary — build day-by-day plans. Updates — post announcements. Share — open the multi-channel share sheet.
trip_dashboard.help_editing_body: You can edit trip details after publishing, but significant changes (date, price, destination) will notify all participants automatically.
trip_dashboard.coach_title: Here are your key tools — manage participants, build the itinerary, post updates, and share your trip.

help.what_is_support_body: Tapping Support on a goal-linked Dream opens a money-transfer flow — you contribute toward the dreamer's savings goal. On a circle-linked Dream, Support becomes "Join" and walks you into the circle.
help.what_is_cheer_title: How do I cheer or comment?

help.who_sees_body: Public Dreams appear to everyone. Communities-only Dreams are visible only to members of the communities you've joined. Anonymous Dreams appear publicly but hide your name.

partial_contribution.what_happens_title: What happens when you activate flexible payment?
partial_contribution.bullet_1: You pay 50% of your contribution today.
partial_contribution.bullet_2: The insurance pool covers the remaining 50% so the circle stays on track.
partial_contribution.bullet_3: You repay the pool over the next 2 cycles (25% each).
partial_contribution.bullet_4: Your XnScore is not penalized for the partial payment.
partial_contribution.fee_notice: The ${{amount}} fee covers the cost of administering the catch-up schedule and the partial coverage from the insurance pool.
partial_contribution.cancel_alert_body: Are you sure you want to cancel and pay the remaining amount now?
partial_contribution.help_why_split_body: The 50/25/25 split covers half the contribution immediately and spreads the rest over the next two cycles so the catch-up amounts stay small and manageable.
partial_contribution.help_missed_catch_up_title: What happens if I miss a catch-up payment?
partial_contribution.help_missed_catch_up_body: Missed catch-up payments affect your XnScore and may require opening a new plan to recover. You'll get reminders before each due date so you can avoid missing one.
partial_contribution.help_pool_covers_title: Why does the insurance pool cover the gap?
partial_contribution.help_pool_covers_body: The pool covers the shortfall so the circle stays on track while you catch up. If the circle has no pool, the shortfall is absorbed by the circle until your catch-up is paid.
partial_contribution.help_fee_when_body: The fee applies on your second and subsequent partial uses in a rolling 12-month window. Your first use each year is free.
partial_contribution.help_xnscore_protection_title: How does my XnScore stay protected?
partial_contribution.help_xnscore_protection_body: Your score is not penalised for the partial payment itself, as long as you stick to the catch-up schedule. Missed catch-ups apply normal late penalties.
partial_contribution.coach_tip: Flexible payment lets you pay 50% now and catch up over the next 2 cycles. Your XnScore is protected as long as you follow the schedule.
partial_contribution.fee_explainer_body: The $10 fee covers the cost of administering the catch-up schedule and the partial coverage from the insurance pool. Your first use each year is free.
partial_contribution.coverage_fallback_no_pool: This circle has no insurance pool. The shortfall is absorbed by the circle until your catch-up is paid.
partial_contribution.what_happens_bullet_1_explainer: You pay the first 50% now. The remaining 50% is split into two catch-up payments due in the next two cycles.
partial_contribution.what_happens_bullet_2_explainer: The insurance pool covers the shortfall so the circle isn't affected. If no pool exists, the circle absorbs the gap until you catch up.
partial_contribution.what_happens_bullet_3_explainer: Catch-up 1 is due next cycle. Catch-up 2 is due the cycle after that. You'll receive reminders before each due date.
partial_contribution.what_happens_bullet_4_explainer: Your XnScore is not penalised for the partial payment, as long as you complete the catch-up schedule on time.

xnscore_dashboard.empty_subtext: Start using TandaXn to build your score!
xnscore_dashboard.recovery_link_text: You have unresolved defaults. Tap to resolve.
xnscore_dashboard.help_what_is_xnscore_body: Your XnScore is a 0–100 trust rating that grows with every contribution you make on time and every circle you complete. It opens larger circles, lower risk slots, and Elder-tier features as it climbs.
xnscore_dashboard.help_five_factors_body: Your score is split across five factors: payment reliability (35%), circle completion (25%), tenure & activity (15%), community standing (15%) and financial behavior (10%). Each factor caps independently, so balanced behavior beats grinding any one of them.
xnscore_dashboard.help_age_cap_body: Score events older than 6 months count for less, and after 18 months they stop counting at all. This keeps your score reflective of recent behavior rather than ancient history.
xnscore_dashboard.help_velocity_cap_body: You can only gain up to 5 points per 24 hours from positive events. This stops anyone from gaming the score through a sudden burst of activity. Big gains land gradually over a few days.
xnscore_dashboard.help_recovery_multiplier_body: After a default, the next 3 on-time contributions are worth 1.5× the usual points to help you climb back. This expires automatically once your streak is rebuilt.
xnscore_dashboard.help_tier_flip_body: Tiers (Newcomer → Trusted → Elder → Elite) unlock progressively as your score rises. Crossing a threshold downward also drops you a tier — features lock at the moment your score dips below the floor.
xnscore_dashboard.coach_tip: Tap (?) on any factor to see what it measures and how to improve it.
xnscore_dashboard.factor_explainer_payment_reliability_bod: Counts how often you contribute on or before the due date. Late and missed contributions reduce it; the recovery multiplier helps you climb back faster after a default.
xnscore_dashboard.factor_explainer_circle_completion_body: Counts every cycle you complete from start to finish without dropping out or being substituted. Voluntarily leaving mid-cycle reduces this factor.
xnscore_dashboard.factor_explainer_tenure_activity_body: Rewards continuous activity in TandaXn. Long stretches of inactivity slowly decay this factor; rejoining and contributing again rebuilds it.
xnscore_dashboard.factor_explainer_community_standing_body: Reflects how the community sees you — vouches, dispute outcomes, and Elder evaluations all feed in. Open disputes can hold this factor down until resolved.
xnscore_dashboard.factor_explainer_financial_behavior_body: Looks at wallet usage patterns: contributing from a funded wallet, paying providers on time, and avoiding chronic overdrafts all lift this factor.
xnscore_dashboard.tip_no_data: You're hitting every factor cap. Stay consistent to maintain your tier.

stress_score.section_contributing_subtitle: 4 signals weighted to calculate your score
stress_score.help_placeholder_body: The Stress Score is a 0–100 prediction of financial pressure, built from four signals: payment timing, support-ticket language, app activity, and early payout requests. Higher means more stress. A full glossary is coming soon.
stress_score.empty_state: We don't have enough data yet. Your stress score will appear after your first contribution or support ticket.
stress_score.top_stressor_body: This is the signal contributing most to your score right now. Tap for details and a suggested next step.
stress_score.suggestions_subtitle: We don't have an active support plan for you yet, but here's where to start.
stress_score.suggestion_contribution_delay_title: Make your next contribution on time
stress_score.suggestion_contribution_delay_body: Topping up before the due date lowers payment-timing stress immediately.
stress_score.suggestion_ticket_language_body: Our support team can help you sort out what's worrying you. Conversations stay private.
stress_score.suggestion_login_drop_body: Checking in regularly helps you spot issues early and shows engagement.
stress_score.suggestion_early_payout_request_body: If you're often requesting early payouts, a small bridging advance may be calmer than juggling cycles.
stress_score.tip_contribution_delay_body: Make your next contribution before the due date to lower stress.
stress_score.tip_ticket_language_body: Talking to support before things escalate keeps your stress signal calm.
stress_score.tip_login_drop_body: Regular logins show engagement and reduce the activity-drop signal.
stress_score.tip_early_payout_request_body: Frequent early payouts signal pressure. A planned liquidity advance can be calmer.
stress_score.help_what_is_stress_score_body: The Stress Score is a 0–100 prediction of financial pressure. It is separate from XnScore (financial reliability) and Honor (community trust). Higher means more stress — the direction is the opposite of the other two scores on purpose, because stress is something we want to flag and reduce, not maximise.
stress_score.help_four_signals_body: Your score is built from four weighted signals: payment timing (30%), support-ticket language (35%), app activity (20%), and early payout requests (15%). Ticket language carries the most weight because the words you use when you ask for help are the most direct signal of real pressure.
stress_score.help_what_we_measure_title: What we measure (and what we don't)
stress_score.help_what_we_measure_body: We measure: when your contributions are paid, how often you open the app, how often you ask for early payouts, and the sentiment of your support tickets. We do NOT read private chats, group chats, dream-feed posts, your wallet balance, or anything outside TandaXn. Your stress score is never shared with elders, circle members or third parties.
stress_score.help_status_colors_body: Green (0–30) is healthy. Yellow (31–60) is mild stress. Orange (61–80) is elevated — this is when we may proactively offer help. Red (81–100) is high stress and triggers our most supportive interventions.
stress_score.help_trend_direction_body: An improving trend uses a downward arrow because your score is going DOWN — and a lower stress score is better. A worsening trend uses an upward arrow. We know it's the opposite of XnScore and Honor; the arrows match the underlying number, not the news.
stress_score.help_interventions_body: When your score crosses into orange or red we may offer a tailored intervention — a restructured payment plan, a small liquidity advance, or a check-in with a counsellor. You can always accept, decline, or ignore. Declining does not affect your XnScore or Honor Score.
stress_score.coach_tip: Higher means more stress — tap any signal to see how it's calculated.
stress_score.signal_explainer_contribution_delay_body: Payment timing measures how often you miss or delay contributions. Late or missed payments raise this signal; on-time and early payments lower it.
stress_score.signal_explainer_contribution_delay_sour: Computed from your contribution records over the last 30 days — late, missed, and partial payments all count toward this signal.
stress_score.signal_explainer_ticket_language_body: Support-ticket language is the strongest single signal because the words you use when asking for help are the most direct measure of pressure. Sentiment analysis runs on tickets you create — never on private chats.
stress_score.signal_explainer_ticket_language_source: Computed from sentiment analysis of support tickets you have created. Private DMs and group chat messages are NOT analysed.
stress_score.signal_explainer_login_drop_body: App activity captures how often you open TandaXn over a rolling window. Steady visits show engagement; sudden drops can mean you're disengaging because of stress.
stress_score.signal_explainer_login_drop_source: Computed from your sign-in events over the last 14 days. We count logins, not the actions you take inside the app.
stress_score.signal_explainer_early_payout_request_bo: Early payout requests track how often you ask for funds before your scheduled cycle. Occasional requests are fine; frequent ones often correlate with cash-flow pressure.
stress_score.signal_explainer_early_payout_request_so: Computed from your advance / early-payout requests over the last 30 days. The amounts don't matter — only the frequency.
stress_score.privacy_note: We collect these signals only to flag financial stress and offer help. We never share your stress score with elders, circle members, or third parties.

mood_insights.privacy_banner: Your mood data is private. Only you can see this.
mood_insights.section_noticing_subtitle: Based on your circle messages (anonymized analysis)
mood_insights.empty_body: Your mood score will appear after you've sent enough circle messages. Keep engaging with your circles.
mood_insights.opt_out_off_sub: Analyzing your circle messages for mood patterns
mood_insights.help_placeholder_body: The Mood Drift Score is a 0–100 estimate of your engagement and tone in circle messages. Higher means more drift away from your usual baseline. We only analyse your own circle messages — never private DMs, never others' messages about you. A full glossary is coming soon.
mood_insights.top_signal_body: This is your biggest mood signal right now. Tap for details and a suggested next step.
mood_insights.suggestions_subtitle: Based on your current mood signals
mood_insights.suggestion_polarity_body: Your recent messages show a more negative tone than usual. A brief reflective post in the Dream Feed may help.
mood_insights.suggestion_lexical_body: Your messages have become noticeably simpler. Joining a community conversation can help re-engage you.
mood_insights.suggestion_keyword_body: We've noticed stress-related language in your recent messages. Our support team can help.
mood_insights.suggestion_latency_body: Replying to circle messages sooner helps you stay connected and is the calmest way to recover this signal.
mood_insights.suggestion_length_body: Short messages can signal disengagement. Sharing a bit more in your next reply helps keep you connected.
mood_insights.tip_polarity_body: A brief reflective post can lift sentiment over the next few days.
mood_insights.tip_lexical_body: A richer message in your next reply gives the model more to work with — and often signals you're re-engaging.
mood_insights.tip_keyword_body: Stress keywords are the strongest single signal. Reaching out early keeps your score calm.
mood_insights.tip_latency_body: Faster replies in your active circles bring this signal down quickly.
mood_insights.tip_length_body: A few extra sentences in your next reply moves this signal back toward your baseline.
mood_insights.help_what_is_mood_score_title: What is the Mood Drift Score?
mood_insights.help_what_is_mood_score_body: The Mood Drift Score is a 0–100 estimate of how much your engagement and tone in circle messages have drifted from your usual baseline. It is separate from XnScore (financial reliability), Honor (community trust) and Stress (financial pressure). Higher means more drift — the direction is the opposite of XnScore and Honor on purpose, because drift is something we want to flag and reduce, not maximise.
mood_insights.help_five_signals_body: Your score is built from five weighted signals: message sentiment (35%), message complexity (25%), stress keywords (15%), response time (15%), and message length (10%). Sentiment carries the most weight because the tone of your words is the most direct signal of how you're doing.
mood_insights.help_what_we_measure_title: What we measure (and what we don't)
mood_insights.help_what_we_measure_body: We measure ONLY your own circle messages — the messages you send into a circle's group chat. We do NOT analyse: private DMs, dream-feed posts, others' messages about you, your wallet activity, your contributions, or anything outside TandaXn. The sentiment analysis runs on our own server with no third-party LLM provider — your words never leave our infrastructure. Most importantly: admins, elders and circle members cannot see your individual mood score. Even our admins only see aggregate trends across cohorts.
mood_insights.help_tiers_body: Stable (0–30) — feeling good, on your baseline. Drifting (31–55) — slight change, worth a look. Disengaging (56–75) — pulling back, this is when we may proactively offer support. At Risk (76–100) — needs support, triggers our most supportive interventions.
mood_insights.help_trend_direction_body: An improving trend uses a downward arrow because your score is going DOWN — and a lower mood-drift score is better (closer to your baseline). A worsening trend uses an upward arrow. We know it's the opposite of XnScore and Honor; the arrows match the underlying number, not the news.
mood_insights.help_interventions_body: When your score crosses into disengaging or at-risk, we may offer a tailored intervention — a check-in nudge, a counsellor referral, or a connection with an elder. Tapping "Connect me with an elder" introduces you to one of our trained community elders for a private chat. Tapping "I'm fine, thanks" closes the offer; nothing changes, no penalty, and your score is not affected by the decision.
mood_insights.coach_tip: Higher means more drift — tap any signal to see how it's calculated.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_polarity_body: Message sentiment measures the emotional tone of your circle messages. Sentiment polarity carries the most weight (35%) because the words you choose are the most direct signal of how you're doing.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_polarity_source: Computed from sentiment polarity of the circle messages you've sent in the current scoring window. Run on our own server — no third-party LLM, no other messages analysed.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_polarity_direction_note: Goes UP when your recent messages skew negative compared to your baseline. Goes DOWN when your tone is closer to neutral or warm.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_lexical_body: Message complexity measures the richness and variety of your vocabulary. A sudden drop in complexity can signal that you're tired, withdrawn, or replying in a hurry.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_lexical_source: Computed from lexical diversity of the circle messages you've sent in the current scoring window (unique words / total words, adjusted for message length).
mood_insights.signal_explainer_lexical_direction_note: Goes UP when your vocabulary suddenly thins relative to your baseline. Goes DOWN when you're writing in your usual richer way.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_keyword_body: Stress keywords picks up specific words that indicate financial or emotional pressure — words like "overwhelmed", "can't", "too much". It's the most actionable signal: if it triggers we want to reach out.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_keyword_source: Computed by matching the circle messages you've sent in the current scoring window against a curated list of stress-related keywords, weighted by severity. The keyword list is reviewed by humans, not generated by an LLM.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_keyword_direction_note: Goes UP when your messages contain more stress-flagged words. Stays at 0 when none are present.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_latency_body: Response time measures how quickly you reply to messages in your active circles. Slowing replies often correlate with disengagement.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_latency_source: Computed from the median time between an inbound message in a circle you're a member of and your reply (capped at 7 days to avoid distorting on long quiet periods).
mood_insights.signal_explainer_latency_direction_note: Goes UP when your replies are taking longer than your baseline. Goes DOWN when you're keeping pace with your circles.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_length_body: Message length measures how much you write per reply. Short, terse replies after a history of fuller messages often signal disengagement or rushed responses.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_length_source: Computed from the average character count of the circle messages you've sent in the current scoring window, compared to your baseline.
mood_insights.signal_explainer_length_direction_note: Goes UP when your messages become noticeably shorter than your baseline. Goes DOWN when you return to your usual length.
mood_insights.privacy_footer: We never share your mood score. Even our admins only see aggregate trends across cohorts, never individual scores. Tap Pause mood analysis above to stop signal collection entirely.

honor_overview.help_placeholder: The Honor Score is your community trust rating from 0–100. It's built from three pillars — Community, Character and Expertise — that grow as you contribute, vouch, complete circles, and resolve disputes. A full glossary is coming soon.
honor_overview.empty_body: Your Honor Score starts forming after your first vouch, completed circle, or resolved case.
honor_overview.section_pillars_subtitle: Each pillar contributes to your total Honor Score. Character is the heaviest — vouching well is the fastest way up.
honor_overview.pillar_expertise_subtitle: Strength of your top-3 skill domains (max 30)
honor_overview.tip_focus_pillar_community_body: Your Community pillar is the weakest right now. Join and complete one more circle — it lifts both circles participation and community engagement.
honor_overview.tip_focus_pillar_character_body: Your Character pillar is the weakest right now. Vouch for someone trustworthy: +5 if they complete the circle, −10 if they default — so choose carefully.
honor_overview.tip_focus_pillar_expertise_body: Your Expertise pillar is the weakest right now. Complete a training course in one of your skill domains to raise your top-3 domain average.
honor_overview.tip_vouches_body: Successful vouches lift your Character pillar; defaults pull it down twice as hard. Quality beats quantity.
honor_overview.tip_mediation_body: Successfully resolved cases earn the strongest single-event Character gain.
honor_overview.tip_training_body: Every completed course raises your Expertise pillar's top-3 domain average.
honor_overview.help_what_is_honor_score_body: The Honor Score is a 0–100 reputation rating built from your behaviour as a circle member, a voucher and a community participant. It's separate from XnScore: XnScore measures financial reliability, Honor measures community trust.
honor_overview.help_three_pillars_body: Your score sums three pillars: Community (max 30), Character (max 40), Expertise (max 30). Character is the heaviest because vouching for others is the strongest signal of community trust you can send.
honor_overview.help_vouching_works_body: When someone you vouched for completes a circle, you earn up to +5 points. When they default, you lose 10 — twice as much. Vouching is asymmetric on purpose: a careless voucher hurts the system more than a careful one helps it.
honor_overview.help_disputes_affect_body: Filing a dispute is fine and is not penalised. Being the subject of a dispute filed against you lowers your Character pillar until the case is resolved; a successful resolution restores most of the points.
honor_overview.help_expertise_domains_body: Expertise is the average of your three strongest skill domains, capped at 30. Completing trainings in a new domain lifts the average — but only if it cracks the top 3.
honor_overview.help_tier_ladder_body: Novice (0–24), Trusted (25–49), Respected (50–74), Elder (75–89), Grand Elder (90–100). Elder unlocks vouching for higher-risk members, dispute mediation, and access to elder rewards. Grand Elder unlocks platform-level governance.
honor_overview.coach_tip: Tap any pillar to see what's inside and how to improve.
honor_overview.pillar_explainer_community_body: Community measures how active you are inside circles (circles participation) and across the wider TandaXn community (community engagement, posts, support, group chat). Each sub-component caps at 15 for a total of 30.
honor_overview.pillar_explainer_character_body: Character is the heaviest pillar (max 40) and captures your trust signals: vouches you've given (max 20), vouches you've received (max 10), and your behaviour in disputes (max 10). A vouched member who defaults costs you twice what a successful vouch gains.
honor_overview.pillar_explainer_expertise_body: Expertise is the average of your three strongest skill domains (max 30) plus a small bonus for breadth across active domains. Complete trainings in a domain to lift its score; only the top-3 average matters.
honor_overview.tip_pillar_community_title: Join and complete one more circle
honor_overview.tip_pillar_community_body: Both Community sub-components — circles participation and community engagement — rise as you join, contribute, and stay active in circles.
honor_overview.tip_pillar_character_body: Pick someone you trust to complete a circle — a successful vouch is the largest single Character gain. Defaults hurt twice as much, so choose carefully.
honor_overview.tip_pillar_expertise_body: Finish a course in one of your skill domains. If it raises a domain into your top three, your Expertise pillar lifts immediately.

credit_profile.empty_loans_body: When you take a loan, it will appear here with its repayment progress and next-due date.
credit_profile.help_placeholder_body: This screen shows your loan history (borrowed, repaid, outstanding, defaults), your active loans with repayment progress, and your upcoming payments. The eligibility card at the top reflects what you could borrow today based on your XnScore.
credit_profile.help_what_is_credit_profile_body: Your complete loan history. See what you've borrowed, repaid, and what you still owe — plus your upcoming payment dates.
credit_profile.help_how_outstanding_body: Outstanding = principal + accrued interest + fees not yet paid. Pay this down to free up new borrowing capacity.
credit_profile.help_what_is_default_title: What counts as a default & how it affects XnScore
credit_profile.help_what_is_default_body: A default lowers your XnScore by 15–30 points and locks you out of new advances for 90 days. Resolve by paying the past-due amount.
credit_profile.help_payment_schedule_body: Each loan has fixed monthly payments. Grace period: 5 days. After day 6, a $5 late fee + 0.5% APR bump apply.
credit_profile.help_dispute_payment_body: Contact support if you believe a payment was misapplied or recorded incorrectly. Provide the loan ID and the transaction date.
credit_profile.coach_tip: Your loan history at a glance. Tap any loan to see details, or use 'Apply' to request a new loan.
credit_profile.tooltip_borrowed: Total amount you've borrowed across all loans.
credit_profile.tooltip_repaid: Total amount you've repaid (principal + interest + fees).
credit_profile.tooltip_outstanding: Total amount still owed (principal + accrued interest + fees).
credit_profile.tooltip_defaults: Number of loans that have defaulted. A default can affect your XnScore.
credit_profile.status_explainer_active_body: Your loan is in good standing. Keep up with payments to avoid late fees.
credit_profile.status_explainer_paid_off_body: This loan has been fully repaid. Well done!
credit_profile.status_explainer_defaulted_body: This loan is past due. Contact support to arrange a repayment plan.
credit_profile.status_explainer_in_collections_body: This loan has been sent to collections. Urgent action required.
credit_profile.status_explainer_written_off_body: This loan has been written off. It no longer accrues interest, but it still affects your XnScore history.
credit_profile.empty_loans_with_cta_body: Your XnScore qualifies you for up to {{amount}}. Apply now to get started.

help.topic_what_vs_event: What's a gathering vs an event?

help.topic_add_to_memory: What does Add to Memory do?
help.topic_add_to_memory_desc: Pinning to your community's Memory keeps the gathering visible in the timeline after it ends, so newer members can see what's happened before.
help.topic_who_sees_desc: Members of this community see it in the Gatherings list and on the Community tab. Circle-type gatherings are highlighted to circle members first.
help.topic_rsvp_desc: Members can say they're going (RSVPs are tracked) so you know roughly how many to plan for. A richer attendee list view is coming soon.

circle_voting.help_what_is_proposal_body: A proposal is a formal request to change something about the circle — a rule, the payout order, or who's a member. Once it opens, every eligible member can vote.
circle_voting.help_who_can_create_body: By default, any active circle member can create a proposal. Some circles restrict proposal creation to admins or the creator — set per circle in the governance settings.
circle_voting.help_six_types_body: Admit a member, Remove a member, Change rules (contribution / grace period / frequency), Change payout order, Dissolve circle, or Custom (free text). Remove and Dissolve are CRITICAL — they require a higher threshold.
circle_voting.help_quorum_vs_threshold_body: Quorum is the minimum number of members who must cast a vote for the result to count. Threshold is the percentage of YES among decisive votes (yes + no) needed to pass. Abstentions count toward quorum, not toward the threshold.
circle_voting.help_voting_period_body: Proposals stay open for the circle's default voting window (48 hours by default). After the window closes, the result is tallied automatically and the proposal moves to the Completed tab.
circle_voting.help_what_happens_on_approval_body: When automation is enabled, approved proposals are executed automatically — circle settings update, members are added or removed, payout order changes. When automation is off, the proposal stays Approved but pending until an admin executes it.
circle_voting.coach_tip: Create a proposal to change circle rules, admit members, or more. Cast your vote on open proposals — your circle decides together.
circle_voting.type_explainer_admit_member_body: Invite a new member into the circle. The vote runs at the standard threshold. When approved (with auto-execution on), the member is added and starts contributing next cycle.
circle_voting.type_explainer_remove_member_body: Remove an existing member from the circle. CRITICAL — requires a higher threshold. The removed member's outstanding obligations are settled per the circle's removal policy.
circle_voting.type_explainer_change_rules_body: Change the contribution amount, grace period, or frequency. When approved (with auto-execution on), the new value applies starting next cycle.
circle_voting.type_explainer_change_payout_order_body: Reorder who receives the pot next. Useful when a member needs the payout earlier for an emergency.
circle_voting.type_explainer_dissolve_circle_body: Wind down the circle. CRITICAL — requires a higher threshold. All outstanding balances are settled per the dissolution policy.
circle_voting.type_explainer_custom_body: Free-text proposal with your own title and description. Approval is recorded but no action is automatically executed — useful for non-binding council decisions.
circle_voting.quorum_explainer_body: Quorum is the minimum number of members who must cast a vote for the result to count. Without quorum, the proposal closes as "No quorum" regardless of how many were for or against. Abstentions count toward quorum.
circle_voting.threshold_explainer_body: Threshold is the percentage of YES among decisive votes (yes + no) needed to pass. Abstentions are not counted in the threshold. Critical proposals (Remove member, Dissolve circle) use a higher threshold than the default.
circle_voting.detail_not_found_body: We couldn't find this proposal — it may have been cancelled or deleted.
circle_voting.detail_no_votes_yet: No votes have been cast yet.
circle_voting.cancel_confirm_body: "{{title}}" will be cancelled and removed from the open list. Members who've already voted will see it move to Completed with a cancelled status.

position_swap.help_body_cooling_off: Once your target accepts, a 24-hour cooling-off period begins. This is an anti-coercion safeguard — neither side can be pressured to confirm immediately.
position_swap.help_body_elder: Some circles require an Elder to approve the swap before it's executed. You'll see a badge on the request when this applies.
position_swap.skip_cooling_off_hint: Both parties must agree to skip the cooling-off period.

insurance_pool.help_withholding_body: A small percentage of each contribution goes to the pool. The rate is set automatically based on your circle's risk profile and stays between 1% and 3%.
insurance_pool.help_missed_payment_title: What happens if I miss a payment?
insurance_pool.help_missed_payment_body: If you miss a contribution, the pool can cover up to 80% of the amount so payouts stay on schedule. You'll be asked to repay the pool through your recovery plan.
insurance_pool.help_money_back_title: When do I get money back?
insurance_pool.help_money_back_body: At the end of each cycle, members vote on whether leftover funds get distributed back to everyone who paid in, or rolled forward to protect the next cycle.
insurance_pool.help_rate_factors_body: The rate is computed from your circle's average XnScore, the lowest member XnScore, how many members are at risk, and the recent default history.

insurance_pool.rate_explainer_description: A lower rate means a healthier, lower-risk circle. The rate goes up automatically when scores drop or defaults stack up, so the pool can absorb the extra risk.
insurance_pool.coach_tip: This pool protects your circle from missed payments. A small share of each contribution goes in; if someone can't pay, the pool covers it.

substitute_pool.alert_leave_body: You won't be offered new substitution matches until you opt back in.
substitute_pool.help_status_modes: Active = ready to be offered substitutions. Standby = contactable but lower priority.
substitute_pool.help_no_cap: 0 means no cap — you can take any circle.
substitute_pool.help_how_pool_body: You opt in once. When a circle anywhere on the network has an open slot, the matching engine sends you an offer. You have 48 hours to accept or decline.
substitute_pool.help_commitment_title: What's the commitment if I accept?
substitute_pool.help_commitment_body: You take the exiting member's slot for the remaining cycles of the circle. You receive up to 80% of the exiting member's payout (insurance pool gets 10%, the exiting member gets 10%).
substitute_pool.help_reliability_body: Starts at 100. Drops by 5 for each decline. Goes up by 1 for each successful substitution (capped at 100). Higher score = higher match priority.
substitute_pool.help_decline_consequences_title: What if I decline 3 times in 90 days?
substitute_pool.help_decline_consequences_body: Your pool entry is automatically suspended for a cooldown period. Suspension lifts once the 90-day window rolls past your oldest decline.
substitute_pool.coach_tip: This pool helps circles stay complete when a member leaves. Join as a substitute to be matched when a slot opens.
substitute_pool.reliability_formula: Starts at 100. Drops 5 points per decline. Gains 1 point per successful substitution (max 100).
substitute_pool.reliability_tier_reliable_desc: Standard match priority. One or two declines won't drop you out.
substitute_pool.reliability_tier_on_notice_desc: Lower priority. Three declines in 90 days will suspend you.
substitute_pool.decline_budget_explainer: You can decline up to 3 substitution offers in any rolling 90-day window. After 3 declines, your pool entry is suspended until the oldest decline rolls past 90 days.

cycle_timeline.empty_no_members_body: Member contributions will appear here as they're scheduled.
cycle_timeline.banner_substitute_title: You have been offered a substitution
cycle_timeline.banner_substitute_body: You've been asked to substitute for a member this cycle. Please confirm or decline.
cycle_timeline.banner_admin_body: Some members have requested substitutions. Please review and approve.
cycle_timeline.substitution_explainer_body: This member has been substituted out for this cycle. The substitute takes their place in the payout queue.
cycle_timeline.help_substitution_body: If a member cannot contribute this cycle, they may be substituted out. Another member takes their place in the payout queue. Substitutions require confirmation from the substitute and approval from the circle admin. You'll see a banner if action is needed.
cycle_timeline.help_what_is_cycle_body: A cycle is one round of the circle. Every active member contributes a fixed amount by the deadline, and one member (the recipient) receives the pooled payout. The circle runs through N cycles in total — one per member.
cycle_timeline.help_what_statuses_mean_body: Pending: not paid yet. Partial: paid some, more owed. Completed: paid on time. Late: paid after the deadline. Missed: never paid (default). Excused: an admin waived the contribution. Covered: the insurance pool paid for the member.
cycle_timeline.help_deadline_vs_grace_body: Contributions are due on the deadline date. If a member misses it, a grace period (set by the circle) gives them a few extra days. After grace ends, unpaid contributions are recorded as missed and the cycle moves toward payout.
cycle_timeline.help_who_receives_payout_body: Each cycle has one recipient, set by the payout order (random or chosen at circle setup). When the cycle is ready to pay out, the recipient gets the pooled amount minus any platform fee. The order can be changed mid-circle by a member vote.
cycle_timeline.help_missed_contribution_title: What if I miss my contribution?
cycle_timeline.help_missed_contribution_body: You'll be marked late during the grace period — you can still pay then. After grace ends, the contribution is recorded as missed, your XnScore takes a hit, and the missed amount may be covered by the insurance pool (if the circle has one). Repeated misses can trigger a member-removal vote.
cycle_timeline.help_cycle_closes_title: What happens when a cycle closes?
cycle_timeline.help_cycle_closes_body: The recipient is paid, the cycle is marked closed, and the next cycle starts. The timeline updates automatically — you can scroll back through past cycles, see who paid when, and tap into any cycle for the full breakdown.
cycle_timeline.coach_tip: Track each cycle's contributions and payouts. Tap a cycle dot to see full details.
cycle_timeline.empty_no_cycles: No cycles have been scheduled yet for this circle.
cycle_timeline.status_explainer_pending_body: The member has not paid this cycle's contribution yet. They can still pay before the deadline without any penalty.
cycle_timeline.status_explainer_partial_body: The member has paid part of the expected amount but still owes the rest. They can top up to mark this contribution as completed.
cycle_timeline.status_explainer_completed_body: The member paid the full amount on or before the deadline. Their on-time rate goes up and their XnScore benefits from the streak.
cycle_timeline.status_explainer_late_body: The member paid after the contribution deadline. The contribution still counts, but their on-time rate dips and a small late fee may apply.
cycle_timeline.status_explainer_missed_body: The member did not pay during the cycle or its grace period. A default is recorded on their history; the missed amount may be covered by the insurance pool.
cycle_timeline.status_explainer_excused_body: An admin waived this contribution for the member — usually for a documented hardship. The contribution counts as resolved without a payment.
cycle_timeline.status_explainer_covered_body: The insurance pool paid the member's contribution this cycle. The cycle still proceeds; the member's balance with the pool is adjusted accordingly.
cycle_timeline.detail_not_found_body: We couldn't find this cycle. It may have been removed or hasn't been created yet.
cycle_timeline.detail_events_empty: No events recorded for this cycle yet.

conflict_alert.no_circle_body: Join or create a circle to see its conflict signals here.
conflict_alert.pick_subtitle: Choose a circle to see its conflict signals and history.
conflict_alert.formation_review_alert_body: Are you sure you want to {{outcome}} this formation?
conflict_alert.empty_no_live_signals_body: This circle is healthy right now. We'll surface formation reviews, monitored pairs, and disputes here as they arrive.
conflict_alert.history_empty_body: No conflicts have been recorded for this circle.
conflict_alert.help_live_vs_dispute_title: Live signal vs. dispute — what's the difference?
conflict_alert.help_live_vs_dispute_body: A Live signal is an AI-flagged pattern: pre-cycle formation flags or mid-cycle monitors the engine surfaces from contribution and behaviour data. A Dispute is member-reported, or auto-created from a missed contribution. Both land in this tab so you can act early.
conflict_alert.help_tier_levels_title: What do Watch, Flag, and Separate mean?
conflict_alert.help_tier_levels_body: Watch: keep an eye on this pair, no action yet. Flag: a friction pattern is forming and is likely to escalate without intervention. Separate: high risk — elder action is recommended, including potentially reassigning the pair to different cycles.
conflict_alert.help_why_autoflag_title: Why does the system auto-flag pairs?
conflict_alert.help_why_autoflag_body: The engine looks for friction patterns — late contributions, communication-style mismatches, and prior disputes — to surface pairs that risk slowing down a cycle. Early visibility lets an elder intervene before the friction spreads.
conflict_alert.help_when_escalate_body: A monitor escalates when the risk score stays elevated over multiple cycles, or when the score crosses the high threshold. An escalated monitor needs elder review — it's the engine saying "this didn't self-resolve."
conflict_alert.help_role_actions_title: What can I do as an elder, admin, or creator?
conflict_alert.help_role_actions_body: Elders can mediate disputes and resolve conflicts. Admins and creators can override formation flags and manage open disputes. Regular members can report a new dispute but cannot resolve one.
conflict_alert.help_status_lifecycle_title: What's the difference between Open, Investigating, and Resolved?
conflict_alert.help_status_lifecycle_body: Open: a dispute was reported but no elder has picked it up yet. Investigating: an elder is reviewing the case and gathering context. Resolved: the case is closed with a final decision recorded on each party's history.
conflict_alert.coach_tip: This screen shows live signals and disputes for this circle. Use the tabs to see what's happening now and what's been resolved. Elders can take action on each item.
conflict_alert.tier_explainer_clear_body: No friction signals detected for this pair. The formation is safe to proceed. No action needed.
conflict_alert.tier_explainer_compatible_body: Engine considers this pair healthy. Low friction predicted across the cycle. No action needed.
conflict_alert.tier_explainer_watch_body: Minor signals detected — keep an eye on this pair. No immediate action needed, but watch their cycle progress.
conflict_alert.tier_explainer_flag_body: A friction pattern is forming and is likely to escalate. Recommend a check-in with both members before the cycle starts.
conflict_alert.tier_explainer_separate_body: High friction risk. Elder action recommended — consider reassigning the pair to different cycles before formation completes.
conflict_alert.severity_explainer_low_body: Minor issue. Often self-resolves. Track for context but no urgent action expected.
conflict_alert.severity_explainer_medium_body: Worth attention. Elders should review and decide whether to mediate.
conflict_alert.severity_explainer_high_body: Likely impacts the cycle. Mediate within 48h to keep the circle on track.
conflict_alert.severity_explainer_critical_body: Risk of cycle failure. Escalate to council if you can't resolve directly.
conflict_alert.review_confirm_body_approved: The pairings will proceed and the cycle can start. You can still mediate later if friction emerges.
conflict_alert.review_confirm_body_override: You'll keep the existing pairings despite the engine warning. Use this when local context beats the model's signal.
conflict_alert.review_confirm_body_rejected: The formation will be blocked. The circle creator will need to rebuild the pairings before the cycle starts.
conflict_alert.review_confirm_body_resolve: Mark this conflict as resolved. It will move to the history with today's date and a resolved status.

conflict_case.help_warn_body: Issue a formal warning. Two warnings trigger an automatic penalty review.
conflict_case.help_mediate_body: Open a free-text ruling and explanation. Both parties must agree before it lands.
conflict_case.help_escalate_body: Forward the case to the council. Use this when you can't reach a fair ruling alone.
conflict_case.help_resolve_body: Close the dispute with a final decision. This stays on each member's history.

elder_onboarding.faq_intro_body: These are the questions every elder gets asked. Tap any row to expand.
elder_onboarding.faq_responsibilities_a: Elders mediate disputes, vouch for new members, and keep their circles aligned with TandaXn rules. Most cases close in under 5 days.
elder_onboarding.faq_case_load_q: How many cases will I handle?
elder_onboarding.faq_case_load_a: Junior Elders take up to 3 cases at a time. Senior and Grand Elders can take more. You always choose which cases to accept.
elder_onboarding.faq_training_path_q: How do I move up tiers?
elder_onboarding.faq_training_path_a: Earn training credits by completing cases, attending council reviews, and maintaining a high success rate. Senior tier opens at 100 credits + 20 cases.
elder_onboarding.faq_earnings_a: Each resolved case pays $25 or more depending on severity. Grand Elders also receive a monthly stipend.
elder_onboarding.faq_removal_q: Can I be removed as an Elder?
elder_onboarding.faq_removal_a: Yes. Three failed cases, repeated escalations without resolution, or rule violations trigger a council review.
elder_onboarding.faq_appeals_a: Either party can appeal a ruling within 7 days. The council reviews appealed cases together — your ruling history is part of the record.

help.title: About this payment
help.topic_deposit_vs_full_body: Paying the deposit secures your spot on the trip and confirms your registration. Paying the full amount settles the entire trip cost up front so you have nothing left to pay later. You can always come back and pay the balance before the trip starts.
help.topic_processing_fee_body: TandaXn doesn't add a fee on top of the trip price. Stripe processes the card payment securely and charges its standard rate to the trip organizer — never to you.
help.topic_refund_policy_body: Each trip has its own cancellation and refund rules set by the organizer. Check the trip page or message the organizer before paying if you're unsure. Stripe-side disputes are also available if a charge doesn't match what you expected.
help.topic_after_pay_body: Your registration flips to confirmed, the organizer is notified instantly, and you'll see the full itinerary plus any trip updates posted by the organizer. If you only paid the deposit, your remaining balance shows on the My Trip Status screen.

coach.title: Choose how much to pay now — deposit holds your spot, full payment settles everything.

score_hub.honor_help_text: Honor Score (0–100) measures your community standing through vouching success, mediation, dispute handling, and track record. Higher is better.
score_hub.stress_help_text: Stress Score (0–100) measures signs of financial pressure: late contributions, support-ticket tone, login drop-off, and early-payout requests. Lower is better.
score_hub.mood_help_text: Mood Drift (0–100) tracks how you talk about your finances over time, compared to your own baseline. Lower means stable; higher means drifting. Lower is better.
score_hub.ai_help_text: AI Insights explains in plain language why each score moved — which signals contributed, and what you can do next.

ai_insights_screen.header_subtitle: Plain-language explanations for your scoring metrics.
ai_insights_screen.xnscore_explanation: Your XnScore is Good because you have repaid 94% of borrowed amount and have no defaults.

ai_insights_screen.help_what_is_insight_body: Every time one of your scores moves significantly, we save a plain-language explanation here. Tap any score card to open its full dashboard.
ai_insights_screen.help_where_from_body: Insights are generated from your activity: contributions, payouts, vouches, disputes, circle completions, and more. They're powered by our own AI engine — no third-party LLMs.
ai_insights_screen.help_how_to_act_body: Each insight points you to the relevant score dashboard for deeper context. Follow the suggested actions there to improve your score over time.

ai_insights_screen.coach_tip: Tap any card to open that score's dashboard, or 'See full history' for the full timeline.
ai_insights_screen.calc_xnscore: Your XnScore (0–100) is recomputed from payment history, completion rate, deposits, tenure, and engagement. Insights surface when the score crosses a threshold or your tier changes.
ai_insights_screen.calc_honor: Honor Score (0–100) tracks your standing across Community, Character, and Expertise pillars. Insights fire when vouching, mediation, or disputes meaningfully shift your standing.
ai_insights_screen.calc_stress: Stress Score (0–100) blends four signals: upcoming due-date pressure, tone shifts, login drop-off, and missed payments. Lower is better.
ai_insights_screen.calc_mood: Mood Drift (0–100) measures how your contribution sentiment compares to your own baseline. Lower means stable; higher means an emerging shift.

circles_screen.payout_help_body: When your circle is full, each member is assigned a number from 1 to N. The lowest numbers get paid out first; later numbers wait longer cycles. Default ordering is the order people joined, but your circle organiser can run an AI optimisation that re-orders by fairness and stability signals. You can also swap your position with another member who agrees.
